findings.description: Contact form POST has no auth, no rate limit, no email validation. Accepts any name/email/message and relays via Resend from a trusted sender address.
findings.exploit_sketch: Attacker POSTs arbitrary bodies in a loop to spam hello@carelog.app, exhaust Resend quota, and abuse the trusted reply-to header to spoof/phish correspondents. Also no zod parsing of shape — oversized payloads accepted.
findings.fix_hint: Add rateLimit(request, 'contact'), zod schema (email(), max lengths), and consider requiring a captcha or session for unauthenticated submissions.

findings.description: OCR upload uses supabaseAdmin.storage.getPublicUrl() on 'prescription-images' bucket and stores the resulting public URL in ocr_jobs.image_url. Bucket is not declared in migrations — if public (Supabase default on manual create), PHI prescription images are world-readable by URL guess/leak.
findings.exploit_sketch: Uploaded URLs follow pattern orgId/recipientId/timestamp-filename.ext. Any leak (logs, referer, shared via brief content, OCR review JSON) becomes a permanent PHI read. URLs are also not signed/expiring.
findings.fix_hint: Declare prescription-images bucket as private in a migration (mirror care-documents pattern). Replace getPublicUrl with createSignedUrl at serve-time; store storage_path not image_url.

findings.description: care_briefs SELECT policy is `USING (true)` — anyone (including anon) can SELECT any row via supabase-js/anon key, not just via API route.
findings.exploit_sketch: With the public anon key, `supabase.from('care_briefs').select('*')` dumps all briefs including PHI content (recipient name, DOB, medications, journal entries). The API route's share_token check is bypassed by hitting PostgREST directly.
findings.fix_hint: Restrict SELECT to `share_token = current_setting('request.jwt.claims',true)::jsonb->>'share_token'` or drop anon SELECT entirely and serve exclusively through the API with service role.

findings.description: outer_circle_requests SELECT policy is `USING (true)` — all requests readable by anon.
findings.exploit_sketch: Anon client can enumerate every outer-circle request across all orgs, including titles/descriptions that may contain sensitive care context (needed_by dates, request notes).
findings.fix_hint: Require SELECT only via share_token matching, or restrict anon to minimal columns needed by the public view.

findings.description: Brief share-token GET has no rate limit. Endpoint returns full PHI payload (name, DOB, medications, journal entries) keyed only on a token.
findings.exploit_sketch: Token is a UUID/random, but with no rate limit an attacker can brute-force tokens or scrape leaked tokens at high throughput; also no cache-control, so tokens appearing in referer/logs get harvested.
findings.fix_hint: Add rateLimit(request, 'brief/view') and Cache-Control: no-store, private.

findings.description: Brief creation POST has no rate limit.
findings.exploit_sketch: Coordinator account compromise or insider can mass-create briefs (each de-tokenizes identity_vault) to exfiltrate PHI en masse.
findings.fix_hint: Add rateLimit(request, 'brief/create') to match other POST endpoints.

findings.description: Outer-circle claim accepts name/email with no zod validation, no email format check, no length limits, no captcha.
findings.exploit_sketch: Spammer can fill all slots with fake claims, block legitimate helpers, and pollute coordinator inbox with bogus emails; oversized name/note fields can DoS display.
findings.fix_hint: Add zod schema (email().max(254), name.max(100), note.max(500)) and integrate captcha or Resend suppression list on claim.

findings.description: GET /api/invite/[token] returns invitee email to anyone who has the token, with rate limit but no auth.
findings.exploit_sketch: If an invite URL is leaked (e.g. in shared email/slack), anyone can retrieve the associated email address (PII) without any ownership check. Combined with F-005 pattern, invite token enumeration could map emails.
findings.fix_hint: Consider not returning email, or return only a masked form (e.g. 'j***@example.com'). Enforce strong token entropy and short expiry.

findings.description: OCR job GET authorizes by `created_by == user.id` only. A coordinator who did not upload the job cannot view it — but more importantly, there is no org_id check after fetching; if created_by is NULL or equals another user's id from a different org, it still scopes correctly, so low-risk. However mixing with the access model elsewhere (org-scoped) may expose jobs to the original uploader even after org removal.
findings.exploit_sketch: An ex-coordinator removed from the org still passes created_by check and can read OCR job data (raw_text, parsed_data) indefinitely via jobId.
findings.fix_hint: Also verify active membership in job.org_id, not only created_by equality.

findings.description: success_url/cancel_url use `request.headers.get('origin')` which is attacker-controllable. Stripe will redirect to this origin after checkout.
findings.exploit_sketch: Attacker sends a crafted POST with Origin: https://evil.tld, gets a Checkout Session whose success_url points at evil.tld/billing/success?session_id=... — phishes the legitimate customer into leaking session_id or tricks them into a fake success page.
findings.fix_hint: Ignore the request Origin header; use only process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL (or a strict allowlist).

findings.description: debug-session route is gated by NODE_ENV !== 'development'. If NODE_ENV is ever misconfigured in preview/staging, cookie names, user email, and Supabase URL are leaked.
findings.exploit_sketch: Vercel preview deploys sometimes run with NODE_ENV=development; attacker hitting /api/debug-session gets session cookie list + user email, enabling targeted session fixation.
findings.fix_hint: Remove the route before production, or gate on an explicit DEBUG_ROUTES=1 env set only locally.

findings.description: Upload path includes user-supplied filename: `filePath = orgId + '/' + recipientId + '/' + Date.now() + '-' + file.name`. No sanitization of file.name — path traversal (`..`) or unicode tricks can place the object in sibling prefixes.
findings.exploit_sketch: Malicious filename like `../other-org/evil.pdf` may normalize into another org's prefix inside Supabase Storage, enabling cross-org write/overwrite of keys where same-named paths exist.
findings.fix_hint: Replace file.name with a hash or crypto.randomUUID() + extension sanitized via allowlist (as done in documents/upload).

findings.description: Invite email template concatenates inviteUrl directly into HTML string without encoding. The url comes from env + generated token so benign today, but if NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL ever contains user-influenced data, HTML injection is possible.
findings.exploit_sketch: Defensive finding — any future code path that lets a user influence the base URL or token format would yield XSS in the email body.
findings.fix_hint: Use a templating helper that escapes variables; or harden a schema on NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL at boot.
